From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Kenyan long-distance runner (born 1985)

Not to be confused with [Helena Kirop](/wiki/Helena_Kirop "Helena Kirop").

Helah Kiprop

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Kiprop at the 2014 Frankfurt Marathon | |
| Personal information | |

| Height | 160 cm (5 ft 3 in)[[1]](#cite_note-1) |
| Weight | 50 kg (110 lb) |
| Sport | |
| Sport | [Athletics](/wiki/Athletics_(sport) "Athletics (sport)") |
| Event | 5000 m – [marathon](/wiki/Marathon "Marathon") |
| Achievements and titles | |
| Personal best(s) | 5000 m – 15:33.90 (2007) 10,000 m – 33:03.8 (2009) 10 km – 31:19 (2013) [Half marathon](/wiki/Half_marathon "Half marathon") – 67:39 (2013) [Marathon](/wiki/Marathon "Marathon") – 2:27:29 (2014) |
| Medal record  |  |  | | --- | --- | |  | | | Representing  [Kenya](/wiki/Kenya "Kenya") | | | | [World Championships](/wiki/IAAF_World_Championships_in_Athletics "IAAF World Championships in Athletics") | | | | Silver medal – second place | [2015 Beijing](/wiki/2015_World_Championships_in_Athletics "2015 World Championships in Athletics") | [Marathon](/wiki/2015_World_Championships_in_Athletics_%E2%80%93_Women%27s_marathon "2015 World Championships in Athletics – Women's marathon") | | |

**Helah Kiprop Jelagat** (born 7 April 1985) is a [Kenyan](/wiki/Kenya "Kenya") professional [long-distance runner](/wiki/Long-distance_runner "Long-distance runner") who competes in the [half marathon](/wiki/Half_marathon "Half marathon") and [marathon](/wiki/Marathon "Marathon"). Her personal bests for the events are 1:07:39 minutes and 2:21:27 hours, respectively.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

She has won the [Berlin Half Marathon](/wiki/Berlin_Half_Marathon "Berlin Half Marathon"), the [Egmond Half Marathon](/wiki/Egmond_Half_Marathon "Egmond Half Marathon") and the [Zwolle Half Marathon](/wiki/Zwolle_Half_Marathon "Zwolle Half Marathon"). She was the 2014 winner of the [Seoul International Marathon](/wiki/Seoul_International_Marathon "Seoul International Marathon") and came fourth at the [2013 Berlin Marathon](/wiki/2013_Berlin_Marathon "2013 Berlin Marathon").

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Helah_Kiprop&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Career")]

### Early career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Helah_Kiprop&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Early career")]

Kiprop had her first win in a European road race in [Nogent-sur-Marne](/wiki/Nogent-sur-Marne "Nogent-sur-Marne") in 2005, running 32:55 minutes at the local [10K run](/wiki/10K_run "10K run"). She began training with GS Valsugana Trentino, an athletics club in [Trento](/wiki/Trento "Trento"), Italy, and made a successful [half marathon](/wiki/Half_marathon "Half marathon") debut in September 2005, taking third place at the high profile [Lille Half Marathon](/wiki/Lille_Half_Marathon "Lille Half Marathon") with a time of 74:02 minutes. She ran for the club in [track races](/wiki/Track_running "Track running") in 2006 and 2007, but did not have the same levels of performance as she did on the roads.[[3]](#cite_note-Tilas-3) She won the [Corrida di San Geminiano](/w/index.php?title=Corrida_di_San_Geminiano&action=edit&redlink=1 "Corrida di San Geminiano (page does not exist)") 15K race in 2007.[[4]](#cite_note-4) She returned to Kenya in 2008 and at the end of the year won the [Tuskys Wareng Cross Country](/wiki/Tuskys_Wareng_Cross_Country "Tuskys Wareng Cross Country").[[5]](#cite_note-5)

A series of road victories came in 2009. A personal best of 69:29 minutes to win at the [Nice Half Marathon](/wiki/Nice_Half_Marathon "Nice Half Marathon") ranked her in the top forty for the event that year.[[6]](#cite_note-6) She won at the [Merano-Lugundo Half Marathon](/w/index.php?title=Merano-Lugundo_Half_Marathon&action=edit&redlink=1 "Merano-Lugundo Half Marathon (page does not exist)"), the [Cuneo Run](/w/index.php?title=Cuneo_Run&action=edit&redlink=1 "Cuneo Run (page does not exist)") and the 10K de la Provence in [Marseille](/wiki/Marseille "Marseille").[[3]](#cite_note-Tilas-3) She had a close battle with [Meseret Mengistu](/wiki/Meseret_Mengistu "Meseret Mengistu") at the [Marseille-Cassis Classique Internationale](/wiki/Marseille-Cassis_Classique_Internationale "Marseille-Cassis Classique Internationale"), but ended up in second place to her [Ethiopian](/wiki/Ethiopia "Ethiopia") rival.[[7]](#cite_note-7)
Kiprop was mainly a runner-up in 2010, starting at the [Alicante Half Marathon](/w/index.php?title=Alicante_Half_Marathon&action=edit&redlink=1 "Alicante Half Marathon (page does not exist)"), then the [Paderborner Osterlauf](/w/index.php?title=Paderborner_Osterlauf&action=edit&redlink=1 "Paderborner Osterlauf (page does not exist)"), [Hambaurg Alsterlauf](/w/index.php?title=Hambaurg_Alsterlauf&action=edit&redlink=1 "Hambaurg Alsterlauf (page does not exist)") and [Singelloop Utrecht](/wiki/Singelloop_Utrecht "Singelloop Utrecht"). She won the [Poznan Half Marathon](/w/index.php?title=Poznan_Half_Marathon&action=edit&redlink=1 "Poznan Half Marathon (page does not exist)") and the [Sotokoto Safari Half Marathon](/w/index.php?title=Sotokoto_Safari_Half_Marathon&action=edit&redlink=1 "Sotokoto Safari Half Marathon (page does not exist)"), as well as setting a personal best 32:20 minutes to win a 10K in [Oelde](/wiki/Oelde "Oelde"). She did not compete in 2011.[[3]](#cite_note-Tilas-3) She reached new heights in 2012 and was runner-up at the [Berlin Half Marathon](/wiki/Berlin_Half_Marathon "Berlin Half Marathon") in a time of 68:26 minutes, just one second behind [Philes Ongori](/wiki/Philes_Ongori "Philes Ongori").[[8]](#cite_note-8)
A win at the [World Bangalore 10K](/wiki/World_Bangalore_10K "World Bangalore 10K") followed a month after.[[9]](#cite_note-9) In June she won the [Zwitserloot Dak Run](/w/index.php?title=Zwitserloot_Dak_Run&action=edit&redlink=1 "Zwitserloot Dak Run (page does not exist)") in a best of 31:44 minutes and was the victor at the [Zwolle Half Marathon](/wiki/Zwolle_Half_Marathon "Zwolle Half Marathon").[[3]](#cite_note-Tilas-3) She travelled to [South America](/wiki/South_America "South America") for the first time and came third at the [Bogotá Half Marathon](/wiki/Bogot%C3%A1_Half_Marathon "Bogotá Half Marathon").[[10]](#cite_note-10) A close third at the [Delhi Half Marathon](/wiki/Delhi_Half_Marathon "Delhi Half Marathon") in November was her last big road race that year.[[11]](#cite_note-11)

### Marathon running

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Helah_Kiprop&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Marathon running")]

The 2013 season marked a career breakthrough for Kiprop as she achieved new personal bests and ran in her first major [marathon](/wiki/Marathon "Marathon") race. She began the year with a win at the [Egmond Half Marathon](/wiki/Egmond_Half_Marathon "Egmond Half Marathon").[[12]](#cite_note-12) The [RAK Half Marathon](/wiki/RAK_Half_Marathon "RAK Half Marathon") proved to be a very fast race, with four women under 67 minutes – Kiprop's personal best time of 67:39 minutes for sixth place was the fastest ever recorded for such a low position.[[13]](#cite_note-13) She set out to win at the Berlin Half Marathon, taking an early lead, and was first to finish in 67:54 minutes, reversing her placings with Ongori from the previous year.[[14]](#cite_note-14) Her fast performances earned her an invite to the [2013 Berlin Marathon](/wiki/2013_Berlin_Marathon "2013 Berlin Marathon") and on her debut run she recorded a time of 2:28:02 hours for the distance, ending in fourth place in the top level competition. Her last outing that year was at the first ever [Kochi Half Marathon](/w/index.php?title=Kochi_Half_Marathon&action=edit&redlink=1 "Kochi Half Marathon (page does not exist)"), which she won.[[15]](#cite_note-15)

Kiprop was among the leaders at the 2014 RAK Half Marathon, but fell behind the eventual winner [Priscah Jeptoo](/wiki/Priscah_Jeptoo "Priscah Jeptoo") and finished fourth in 68:36 minutes.[[16]](#cite_note-16) At the 2014 [Seoul International Marathon](/wiki/Seoul_International_Marathon "Seoul International Marathon"), Kiprop topped the podium in the classic distance for the first time. Her run of 2:27:29 hours was a personal best and enough to fend off [Ashu Kasim](/wiki/Ashu_Kasim "Ashu Kasim") in the final kilometres of the race.[[17]](#cite_note-17)

She was coached by her husband, Ugandan [David Marus](/wiki/David_Marus "David Marus").[[18]](#cite_note-18)

In 2017 Kiprop competed in the [marathon](/wiki/2017_World_Championships_in_Athletics_%E2%80%93_Women%27s_marathon "2017 World Championships in Athletics – Women's marathon") at the [2017 World Championships](/wiki/2017_World_Championships_in_Athletics "2017 World Championships in Athletics") held in London, placing 7th in 2:28:19.[[19]](#cite_note-19)

## Achievements

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Helah_Kiprop&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Achievements")]

| [Boston Marathon](/wiki/Boston_Marathon "Boston Marathon") | - | - | - | - | - |  |

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Helah_Kiprop&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: External links")]
